AttributesConfig.description:
Document-specific detection knobs. ``extra='allow'`` is intentional —
this is the one place future flags slot in without a schema change.

Anything added here is purely advisory: stages read it to decide
whether to populate corresponding entries under ``Chunk.attributes``.

detect_building_type.description: When True, tag chunks with applicable building types (e.g. 'dwellinghouse', 'flat') mentioned in the text.
detect_building_type.title: Detect Building Type

detect_requirement_scope.description: When True, tag each chunk with the Building Regulation requirement (B1..B5) inferred from its section context.
detect_requirement_scope.title: Detect Requirement Scope

AttributesConfig.title: AttributesConfig

ChunkingConfig.description: How to split the document into chunks. Controls Stage 2 (``chunk``).

heading_levels.description: Nesting levels the parser should treat as section_heading chunks — e.g. [1, 2, 3, 4].
heading_levels.title: Heading Levels

merge_continuations.description: When True, paragraphs without their own number that follow a numbered paragraph are merged into it as continuations. Disable only if the document has a stricter numbering scheme.
merge_continuations.title: Merge Continuations

paragraph_regex.description: Regex that recognises the start of a numbered body paragraph in this document. Capture group 1 is the paragraph number used to build chunk_ids. Compiled at config-load time — invalid regex fails fast.
paragraph_regex.title: Paragraph Regex

preserve_reading_order.description: When True, chunks are emitted in the parser's reading order (currently always True; the flag is here to make the contract explicit).
preserve_reading_order.title: Preserve Reading Order

treat_captions_as_chunks.description: When True, table/diagram captions are emitted as their own caption-type chunks with bidirectional links to their target. Disable only if you genuinely want captions folded into surrounding paragraphs (lossy).
treat_captions_as_chunks.title: Treat Captions As Chunks

ChunkingConfig.title: ChunkingConfig

ParserConfig.description:
Which parser implementations to use for this document. Defaults
cover digital PDFs; a future OCR parser would slot in by changing the
``primary`` value.

link_extractor.description: Name of the parser that extracts PDF outline and hyperlinks.
link_extractor.title: Link Extractor

primary.description: Name of the structural parser (produces the document tree).
primary.title: Primary

ParserConfig.title: ParserConfig

ReferencePattern.description:
One regex used by Stage 3 to find cross-references in chunk text.

Internal patterns capture a target identifier (e.g. paragraph number)
in group 1; Stage 3 resolves it to a chunk_id. External patterns
typically don't capture — the surface text is normalised into
``external_id``.

name.description: Identifier for this pattern; appears in Reference.pattern_name. Use snake_case — e.g. 'internal_paragraph', 'bs_standard'.
name.title: Name

regex.description: The regex itself. Compiled at config-load time.
regex.title: Regex

type.description: The kind of edge this pattern produces.

ReferencePattern.title: ReferencePattern

ReferenceType.description:
Kind of edge between a chunk and its target.

See ``docs/schemas.md`` §References for guidance on which type to use.
ReferenceType.title: ReferenceType

ReferencesConfig.description: How to find cross-references. Controls Stage 3 (``resolve_references``).

glossary_section.description: Name of the section containing the glossary — e.g. 'Appendix E'. Tells Stage 5 where to find defined terms. None when the document has no glossary.
glossary_section.title: Glossary Section

patterns.description: Regex patterns to run over every chunk's text. PDF hyperlinks are handled separately and always take precedence.
patterns.title: Patterns

ReferencesConfig.title: ReferencesConfig

SourcingConfig.description:
Coordinate convention. Frozen but explicit so downstream tools can
inspect the config rather than guessing.

coordinate_origin.description: Where the bbox origin is. Frozen at 'top_left' for now.
coordinate_origin.title: Coordinate Origin

coordinate_unit.description: Bbox unit. Frozen at 'pt' (PDF points = 1/72 inch).
coordinate_unit.title: Coordinate Unit

SourcingConfig.title: SourcingConfig

ValidationConfig.description:
Thresholds for Stage 6. Each threshold is a 0..1 minimum rate the
corresponding metric must clear for the pipeline to pass.

fail_on_schema_error.description: When True (default), any artifact failing its JSON Schema fails the run. Disable only for debugging.
fail_on_schema_error.title: Fail On Schema Error

min_internal_ref_resolution.description: Minimum fraction of internal references that must resolve to a known chunk. Below this, the run fails.
min_internal_ref_resolution.title: Min Internal Ref Resolution

min_page_coverage.description: Minimum fraction of pages that must have at least one chunk. Catches accidental whole-page misses.
min_page_coverage.title: Min Page Coverage

min_reading_order_monotonicity.description: Per page, chunks sorted by order_index should have non-decreasing y-coordinates this fraction of the time. Catches reading-order regressions.
min_reading_order_monotonicity.title: Min Reading Order Monotonicity

min_text_reconstruction_coverage.description: Walking chunks in order and concatenating their text must cover this fraction of the source PDF's text (whitespace-normalised). The round-trip guarantee.
min_text_reconstruction_coverage.title: Min Text Reconstruction Coverage

ValidationConfig.title: ValidationConfig

description:
Top-level per-document config. One YAML file per document — adding a
new document means writing a new config, not changing code.

doc_id.description: Short, stable identifier for the document — e.g. 'ADB1-2022'. Used as the prefix of every chunk_id and as the output directory name. Must be unique per document.
doc_id.title: Doc Id

edition.description: Edition or year of the document.
edition.title: Edition

source_pdf.description: Path to the source PDF, relative to the repository root. The file itself is gitignored under inputs/.
source_pdf.title: Source Pdf

title: Config
